enonce.texte: Complete the sentences with in order to or so as to to express purpose.

questions.texte: Fill in the blanks with the correct purpose expression.

sousQuestions.texte: I study hard _______ pass my exams.
sousQuestions.reponse: in order to / so as to

sousQuestions.texte: She went to the bank _______ withdraw some money.
sousQuestions.reponse: in order to / so as to

sousQuestions.texte: He took a taxi _______ not be late for the meeting.
sousQuestions.reponse: in order not to / so as not to

sousQuestions.texte: They are saving money _______ buy a new house.
sousQuestions.reponse: in order to / so as to

sousQuestions.texte: We left early _______ avoid the traffic.
sousQuestions.reponse: in order to / so as to

enonce.texte: Distinguish between purpose (in order to / so as to) and reason (because).

questions.texte: Complete the sentences with the correct expression: in order to, so as to, so as not to, in order not to, or because.

sousQuestions.texte: She went to the supermarket _______ buy some groceries.
sousQuestions.reponse: in order to / so as to

sousQuestions.texte: I didn't go to the party _______ I was tired.
sousQuestions.reponse: because

sousQuestions.texte: He wore a jacket _______ keep warm.
sousQuestions.reponse: in order to / so as to

sousQuestions.texte: They spoke quietly _______ disturb the baby.
sousQuestions.reponse: in order not to / so as not to

sousQuestions.texte: We turned off the lights _______ save electricity.
sousQuestions.reponse: in order to / so as to

enonce.texte: Use in order not to or so as not to to express negative purpose.

questions.texte: Complete the sentences with the correct negative purpose expression.

sousQuestions.texte: He left early _______ miss the bus.
sousQuestions.reponse: in order not to / so as not to

sousQuestions.texte: She spoke softly _______ wake the baby.
sousQuestions.reponse: in order not to / so as not to

sousQuestions.texte: They arrived early _______ be late.
sousQuestions.reponse: in order not to / so as not to

sousQuestions.texte: I closed the window _______ let the cold air in.
sousQuestions.reponse: in order not to / so as not to

sousQuestions.texte: He wore a mask _______ spread the virus.
sousQuestions.reponse: in order not to / so as not to

enonce.texte: Rewrite the sentences using in order to or so as to.

questions.texte: Transform these sentences to express purpose.

sousQuestions.texte: She is studying English. She wants to get a better job.
sousQuestions.reponse: She is studying English in order to / so as to get a better job.

sousQuestions.texte: He took a map. He wanted not to get lost.
sousQuestions.reponse: He took a map in order not to / so as not to get lost.

sousQuestions.texte: We arrived early. We wanted to get good seats.
sousQuestions.reponse: We arrived early in order to / so as to get good seats.

sousQuestions.texte: She saved money. She wanted to buy a new phone.
sousQuestions.reponse: She saved money in order to / so as to buy a new phone.

sousQuestions.texte: They wore warm clothes. They didn't want to catch a cold.
sousQuestions.reponse: They wore warm clothes in order not to / so as not to catch a cold.

enonce.texte: Read each situation and write a sentence using in order to or so as to.

questions.texte: Write a purpose sentence for each situation.

sousQuestions.texte: You want to buy a new book. You go to the library.
sousQuestions.reponse: I go to the library in order to / so as to buy a new book.

sousQuestions.texte: You want to not be late. You set an alarm.
sousQuestions.reponse: I set an alarm in order not to / so as not to be late.

sousQuestions.texte: You want to improve your health. You exercise regularly.
sousQuestions.reponse: I exercise regularly in order to / so as to improve my health.

sousQuestions.texte: You want to not forget your keys. You put them in your bag.
sousQuestions.reponse: I put my keys in my bag in order not to / so as not to forget them.

sousQuestions.texte: You want to save energy. You turn off the lights.
sousQuestions.reponse: I turn off the lights in order to / so as to save energy.

enonce.texte: Each sentence contains a mistake with purpose expressions. Identify and correct it.

questions.texte: Correct the mistakes in the following sentences.

sousQuestions.texte: She went to the market for buy vegetables.
sousQuestions.reponse: She went to the market in order to / so as to buy vegetables.

sousQuestions.texte: He left early for not miss the train.
sousQuestions.reponse: He left early in order not to / so as not to miss the train.

sousQuestions.texte: I study hard to pass my exams.
sousQuestions.reponse: I study hard in order to / so as to pass my exams. (This is also correct: 'to' alone can express purpose)

sousQuestions.texte: She took an umbrella so as don't get wet.
sousQuestions.reponse: She took an umbrella so as not to get wet. / in order not to get wet.

sousQuestions.texte: We arrived early in order to not miss the beginning.
sousQuestions.reponse: We arrived early in order not to / so as not to miss the beginning.

enonce.texte: Complete the dialogue using appropriate purpose expressions.

questions.texte: Complete the conversation with in order to, so as to, in order not to, so as not to.

sousQuestions.texte:
A: "Why are you studying so hard?"
B: "I'm studying hard _______ get a scholarship."
sousQuestions.reponse: in order to / so as to

sousQuestions.texte:
A: "Why did you wake up so early?"
B: "I woke up early _______ miss the bus."
sousQuestions.reponse: in order not to / so as not to

sousQuestions.texte:
A: "Why are you saving money?"
B: "I'm saving money _______ buy a new laptop."
sousQuestions.reponse: in order to / so as to

sousQuestions.texte:
A: "Why did you bring an umbrella?"
B: "I brought an umbrella _______ get wet."
sousQuestions.reponse: in order not to / so as not to

sousQuestions.texte:
A: "Why are you going to the gym?"
B: "I'm going to the gym _______ stay fit."
sousQuestions.reponse: in order to / so as to

enonce.texte: You are a community leader planning a development project. Write a short proposal describing the purpose of each action using in order to and so as to.

questions.texte: Write a community development proposal with at least 6 sentences using purpose expressions.
questions.reponse:
Exemple de réponse:

Our community is planning several development projects. We are building a new school in order to provide quality education for our children. We are digging a well so as to ensure access to clean water. We are organizing clean-up campaigns in order not to let the environment become polluted. We are planting trees so as to protect the environment. We are training young people in order to create employment opportunities. We are starting a community farm in order to improve food security. We are building a health center so as to ensure access to healthcare for everyone. We are constructing roads in order to improve transportation. We are organizing workshops so as to raise awareness about hygiene. We are creating a community library in order to promote reading and education.
